fields.name: Buy-from Vendor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the vendor who delivered the items.

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Pay-to Vendor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the vendor that you receiv or received the invoice from.

fields.name: Pay-to Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the vendor who you received the invoice from.

fields.name: Pay-to Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the name of the vendor who you receive or received the invoice from.

fields.name: Pay-to Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address of the vendor that you received the invoice from.

fields.name: Pay-to Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the address of the vendor that the invoice was received from.

fields.name: Pay-to City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city of the vendor that you received the invoice from.

fields.name: Pay-to Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the contact person at the vendor that you received the invoice from.

fields.name: Your Reference
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the vendor's reference.
The program copies the reference from the Your Reference field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the reference because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Ship-to Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for an alternate shipment address if you want to ship to another address than the one that has been entered automatically. This field is also used in case of drop shipment.

fields.name: Ship-to Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the customer at the address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Ship-to Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the name of the customer that items on the purchase order were shipped to, as a drop shipment.

fields.name: Ship-to Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address that items on the purchase order were shipped to, as a drop shipment..

fields.name: Ship-to Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the ship-to address, in case it is a long address.

fields.name: Ship-to City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city of the address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Ship-to Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the contact person at the address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Order Date
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date the purchase header was created.
The program copies the date from the Order Date field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the order date because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the posting date of the record.

fields.name: Expected Receipt Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date you expect the items to be available in your warehouse. If you leave the field blank, it will be calculated as follows: Planned Receipt Date + Safety Lead Time + Inbound Warehouse Handling Time = Expected Receipt Date.

fields.name: Posting Description
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the entry type and number, such as Receipt 22066.
The program copies the posting description from the Posting Description field on the purchase header.
The posting description also appears on vendor and G/L entries.
You cannot change the posting description because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Payment Terms Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code to use to find the payment terms that apply to the purchase header.
The program copies the payment terms code from the Payment Terms Code field on the purchase header.
The payment terms are used to calculate the due date and payment discount date from the document date and to find payment discount percents.
You cannot change the payment terms code because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Due Date
fields.tooltip:
Specifies when the receipt is due. The program calculates the date using the Payment Terms Code and Posting Date fields on the purchase header.
After you post orders, invoices and receipts, the program uses the due date to find the vendors whose bills are due when you run the Suggest Vendor Payments batch job.
You cannot change the due date because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Payment Discount %
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the payment discount percentage that will be given if you pay for the purchase on or before the date in the Pmt. Discount Date field.
The program copies the payment discount percentage from the Payment Discount % field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the payment discount percentage because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Pmt. Discount Date
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the last date on which you can pay the receipt and still receive a payment discount.
The program copies the discount date from the Pmt. Discount Date field on the purchase header.
After you post orders, invoices and receipts, the program uses the due date to find the vendors whose bills are due when you run the Suggest Vendor Payments batch job.
You cannot change the discount date because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Shipment Method Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the delivery conditions of the related shipment, such as free on board (FOB).

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the location where the items on the receipt were registered.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Vendor Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the vendor posting group that was used when the receipt was posted.
The program copies the vendor posting group from the Vendor Posting Group field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the posting group because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the currency code used to calculate the amounts on the receipt.
The program copies the currency code from the Currency Code field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the currency code because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Currency Factor
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Invoice Disc. Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code that will be used to find the invoice discount that was granted on the receipt.
The program copies the invoice discount code from the Invoice Disc. Code field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the invoice discount code because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Language Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the language used on the receipt.
The program copies the code from the Language Code field on the purchase header.
The language code made it possible for the items on the purchase lines to be written in the vendor's language.
You cannot change the code because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Purchaser Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies which purchaser is assigned to the vendor.

fields.name: Order No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line number of the order that created the entry.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether or not a comment has been entered for this purchase receipt header.
To view a comment or create a new one, click Related Information, point to Receipt, and then click Comments. The Comment Sheet window will appear. Here you can see the existing comments and create new ones.
You can also enter a comment in the Notes FactBox.

fields.name: No. Printed
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many times the document has been printed.

fields.name: On Hold
fields.tooltip:
Determines whether the posted receipt will be included in the payment suggestion when the purchase header has been invoiced and you run the Suggest Vendor Payments batch job.
Before posting you can enter your initials or whatever you like in the field. This indicates that it will be put on hold.
If the field is not empty, the entry will not be included when you run the Suggest Vendor Payments batch job. The field will show that the receipt is waiting for approval. If the field is blank, the entry will be included in an automatic payment.
The field can be changed on the vendor ledger entry after posting.

fields.name: Applies-to Doc. Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether the receipt has been applied to an already-posted document. If so, the field shows the type of the document applied to.
The program copies the document type from the Applies-to Doc. Type field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the document type because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Applies-to Doc. No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether the receipt has been applied to an already-posted document. If so, the field contains the number of the document.
The program copies the document number from the Applies-to Doc. No. field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the document number because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Bal. Account No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the general ledger account or bank account to which a balancing entry for the receipt was posted.
The program copies the balancing account number from the Bal. Account No. field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the balancing account number because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Vendor Order No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's order number.

fields.name: Vendor Shipment No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's shipment number. It is inserted in the corresponding field on the source document during posting.

fields.name: VAT Registration No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the vendor's VAT registration number.
The program copies the number from the VAT Registration No. field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the number because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Sell-to Customer No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of your customer to whom the vendor shipped the items directly. This field is used only when the receipt line is for a drop shipment.
The program copies the number from the Sell-to Customer No. field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the number because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Reason Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the reason code that is associated with the receipt.
The program copies the reason code from the Reason Code field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the reason code because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the general business posting group code that is assigned to the vendor that invoiced the shipment.
The program copies the code from the Gen. Bus. Posting Group field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the code because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Transaction Type
fields.tooltip:
Shows the transaction type of the purchase header that this line was posted from.
The program copies the transaction type from the Transaction Type field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the transaction type because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Transport Method
fields.tooltip:
Shows the transport method of the purchase header that this line was posted from.
The program copies the transport method from the Transport Method field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the transport method because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: VAT Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip:
Shows the vendor's VAT country/region code.
The program copies the VAT country/region code from the VAT Country/Region Code field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the VAT country/region code because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Buy-from Vendor Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the vendor who delivered the items.

fields.name: Buy-from Vendor Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the name of the vendor that you’re buying from.

fields.name: Buy-from Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address of the vendor who delivered the items.

fields.name: Buy-from Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the address of the vendor who delivered the items.

fields.name: Buy-from City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city of the vendor who delivered the items.

fields.name: Buy-from Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the contact person at the vendor who delivered the items.

fields.name: Pay-to Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the post code of the vendor that you received the invoice from.

fields.name: Pay-to County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the state, province or county related to the posted purchase order.

fields.name: Pay-to Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region code of the address.

fields.name: Buy-from Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the post code of the vendor who delivered the items.

fields.name: Buy-from County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the state, province or county related to the posted purchase order.

fields.name: Buy-from Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city of the vendor who delivered the items.

fields.name: Ship-to Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the postal code of the address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Ship-to County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the state, province or county related to the posted purchase order.

fields.name: Ship-to Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region code of the address that the items are shipped to.

fields.name: Bal. Account Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the balancing account type used in the receipt.
The program copies the balancing account type from the Bal. Account Type field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the type because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Order Address Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the order address of the related vendor.

fields.name: Entry Point
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code of the port of entry where the items pass into your country/region.
The program copies the code from the Entry Point field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the code because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Correction
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether this purchase receipt has been posted as a corrective entry.
The program copies the code from the Correction field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the code because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Document Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the purchase document was created.

fields.name: Area
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the area code used in the receipt.
The program copies the code from the Area field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the code because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Transaction Specification
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the transaction specification that was used in the receipt.
The program copies the code from the Transaction Specification field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the code because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Payment Method Code
fields.tooltip:
Describes the method of payment for payments to vendors. The program has copied the code from the Payment Method Code field on the purchase header.
You cannot change or delete the code because the purchase receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the number series that was used to assign a number to the purchase receipt.
The program automatically copies this field from the Receiving No. Series field on the purchase header.
You cannot change the number series because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: Order No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the number series that was used to assign a number to the order from which the receipt was created.
The program automatically copies this field from the No. Series field on the purchase order header.
If the receipt was not created from an order, the field is blank.
You cannot change the number series because the receipt has already been posted.

fields.name: User ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the ID of the user who posted the purchase receipt.
You cannot change the user ID because the receipt has been posted.

fields.name: Source Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the source code that is linked to the purchase receipt header.
The program copies the code from either the Purchases field or the Deleted Document field in the Source Code Setup table.
You cannot change the code because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the tax area code of the vendor.
The program copies the code from the Tax Area Code field on the purchase header.

fields.name: Tax Liable
fields.tooltip:
Specifies that purchases from the vendor on the purchase header was liable to sales tax.
The program copies the code from the Tax Liable field on the purchase header.

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the VAT business posting group of the vendor.
The program copies the code from the VAT Bus. Posting Group field on the purchase header.

fields.name: VAT Base Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage to use to discount the VAT element if a payment discount is to be posted. The value is validated against the maximum possible VAT Tolerance % field value in the General Ledger Setup. The maximum value cannot be overridden with the exception of a credit note where you can manually enter the value used for the payment discount.

fields.name: Quote No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quote number for the posted purchase receipt.

fields.name: Ship-to Phone No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the telephone number of the company's shipping address.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


You cannot change the contents of this field. Click the drop-down arrow to see the combination of posted dimension values.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: Campaign No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the campaign that the document is linked to.

fields.name: Buy-from Contact No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the contact person at the vendor who delivered the items.

fields.name: Pay-to Contact no.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the person to contact about an invoice from this vendor.

fields.name: Responsibility Center
fields.tooltip: Specifies the responsibility center code that is linked to this posted return receipt.

fields.name: Requested Receipt Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that you want the vendor to deliver to the ship-to address. The value in the field is used to calculate the latest date you can order the items to have them delivered on the requested receipt date. If you do not need delivery on a specific date, you can leave the field blank.

fields.name: Promised Receipt Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that the vendor has promised to deliver the order.

fields.name: Lead Time Calculation
fields.tooltip: Specifies a date formula for the amount of time it takes to replenish the item.

fields.name: Inbound Whse. Handling Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the time it takes to make items part of available inventory, after the items have been posted as received.
